title: Harness manifest
description: Declares the working set of components a project uses. harness.yaml lives under harness/ (harness/harness.yaml). Two component lists — microskills and workflows — each entry tagged by source. source 'plugin' = materialized from the plugin catalog by `initialize-harness` (referenced, never vendored into harness/). source 'custom' = authored locally under harness/<kind-dir>/<name>/ and reconciled by `harness-sync`. The generated runtime is .claude/; .claude/.harness-state.json is the ownership ledger. Closed grammar: unknown fields at any level are rejected.

version.description: Manifest schema version. Only 2 is recognized.

microskills.description: Microskills this project uses. Deploy target: .claude/microskills/<name>/ (+ generated shim .claude/commands/<name>.md). source 'plugin' read from <catalog>/microskills/<name>/; source 'custom' read from harness/microskills/<name>/.

workflows.description: Workflows this project uses. Deploy target: .claude/workflow-defs/<name>/ (+ generated shim .claude/commands/<name>.md). source 'plugin' read from <catalog>/workflow-defs/<name>/; source 'custom' read from harness/workflow-defs/<name>/.

name.description: Component directory name. Kebab-case; matches the microskill/workflow name regex.

source.description: Provenance and ownership. 'plugin' = pulled from the plugin catalog and materialized into .claude/ by initialize-harness; harness-sync NEVER touches these. 'custom' = authored under harness/ and reconciled into .claude/ by harness-sync.

profiles.description: Which profile overlays to vend for this component. Either an explicit, non-empty, unique list of profile names, or the wildcard string "*" meaning 'all profiles present in the source'. Omit to mean all. For 'plugin' the named overlays are selected from the catalog source; for 'custom' the bytes under harness/.../profiles/ are authoritative and the named overlays must already exist there.

version.description: Optional provenance pin for 'plugin' (which catalog version was materialized). Ignored for 'custom'. The seam for a future upstream-drift check.
